title: pasta networking breaks with passt 2026_07_16 snapshot on Podman 6.0.1 (regression from 2026_01_20)
body:
### Issue Description

I run rootless Podman 6.0.1 (released 2026-07-07) on Ubuntu 26.04, using pasta for networking (default rootless backend). My distro-packaged passt/pasta was from the 2026_01_20.386b5f5 snapshot.

Today I built passt from the (most recent) official 2026_07_16.090d739 source tarball (https://passt.top/passt/snapshot/passt-2026_07_16.090d739.tar), packaged it as a .deb, and installed it in place of the older build. After this upgrade, pasta/passt networking for my Podman containers started failing. Containers that were running stayed running, but those that were stopped couldn't restart with the new pasta/passt with the following error message - 
```
Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:

Couldn't open network namespace /run/user/1000/netns/netns-67ad4bef-95e8-f72e-ec61-eb62abf96884: Permission denied
```
I stopped all containers and networks, rebooted the computer, and started up the networks and then tried to start up the containers again.  Same error.

Reverting to the 2026_01_20.386b5f5 build restored normal operation without needing a reboot or needing to restart .networks.  I haven't tried the June 2026 pasta/passt version on passt.top yet to see if this worked or throws a similar error.

Podman 6.0.1 was released before the 2026_07_16 passt snapshot existed, so it's possible this newer passt build changed something in the fd-passing/isolation contract that Podman's pasta invocation relies on, that Podman 6.0.1 wasn't tested against. I haven't yet bisected the exact commit between the January and July snapshots responsible but over the past 1-2 weeks the git commits on passt alludes to changes in fd.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. download July 2026 passt from passt.top
2. compile it
3. replace older pasta/passt with the july 2026 version
4. stop running containers and try to restart


### Describe the results you received

```
Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:

Couldn't open network namespace /run/user/1000/netns/netns-67ad4bef-95e8-f72e-ec61-eb62abf96884: Permission denied
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the container to start up normally

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

run on ubuntu 26.04 on a irl computer

### Additional information

I suspect the fd-passing/isolation handling changed somewhere in passt between these two snapshots and I'm flagging this mainly so the Podman and passt maintainers can coordinate on compatibility, since I don't have the bandwidth to bisect the exact commit myself. Passt wasn't letting me create a new bugzilla account (I think this is ironically a bugzilla bug) so I haven't been able to notify them.

comments.body:
In general this kind of issue is a security policy problem not a really related to podman. In most cases it is selinux causes issues for us but because you use ubuntu it is apparmor there. My guess would be that same security rules have changed and/or need to be adapted there.
I do not believe this has anything to do with podman itself.

It would help how you actually build the deb, are you actually including the up to date profiles from the repo https://passt.top/passt/tree/contrib/apparmor and make sure they work.

cc @sbrivio-rh 

> I suspect the fd-passing/isolation handling changed somewhere in passt between these two snapshots and I'm flagging this mainly so the Podman and passt maintainers can coordinate on compatibility, since I don't have the bandwidth to bisect the exact commit myself. Passt wasn't letting me create a new bugzilla account (I think this is ironically a bugzilla bug) so I haven't been able to notify them.

The emails require manual confirmation by the maintainers to avoid the spam problem I think

Hi.  Sorry to leave that out. I downloaded the .tar directly, then
```
sudo checkinstall \
  --pkgname=passt \
  --pkgversion="0.0~git20260716.090d739" \
  --pkgrelease="1" \
  --provides="passt,pasta" \
  --requires="libc6" \
  --nodoc \
  --default \
  make install prefix=/usr
```
using the `checkinstall` in the Ubuntu 26.04 repo.


If I missed a step and this is user error that would make me feel better. I
saw the error thrown by pasta upon starting a container and saw recent
commits to fd in passt both before and after passt 2026-07-16 was released
and I figured it was a breaking change between pasta/passt and podman
6.0.1.

But user error with AppArmor would be a much welcomed explanation.

Ty.

User error!  @Luap99 

I added app armor, passt starts.  Sorry to be a PITA.  I got it fixed.  will close .  Appreciate your help.  hopefulyl either when I screw i up again or if someone else makes the same rookie mistake they will see this issue and not bug you guys.

> Passt wasn't letting me create a new bugzilla account (I think this is ironically a bugzilla bug) so I haven't been able to notify them.

Hey, sorry, but you sent two requests with 104 seconds between them. I approved them within 13 minutes, I wouldn't exactly call "not letting me create an account". It's not a Bugzilla issue by the way, as @Luap99 pointed out: I need to approve those manually because we have LLM "agents" desperately trying to register accounts there.

Anyway, should anybody land here in the future hitting the same issue: full details at https://bugs.passt.top/show_bug.cgi?id=218#c1.

yeah, that was my fault too. I think the emails got hung up between you
guys and my gmail accounts and it took about 5 min or more to arrive.  And
then of course ended up in their spam filter. I was able to create the
bugzilla account.  I appreciate the help.
